title: Key Derivation, HKDF, and Key Separation · Runtime Contract: Crypto Go Transcript Binding
summary: A protocol slices one shared secret into keys, uses user passwords as HKDF input, omits transcript context, and changes output length without versioning. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the transcript binding increment in labeled key schedule and derivation tree. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and eviden…
objectives: Bind negotiated identities, algorithms, public values, roles, and transcript hashes into derivation context.
Derive independent role, direction, epoch, algorithm, and purpose keys from a versioned hierarchy without key cycles.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Transcript Binding · Theory Crypto Go Transcript Binding
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 8868 during a replicated device restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive bounded purpose- and role-separated HKDF keys bound to the negotiated transcript; retain exact canonical bytes, purpose, role, version, and artifact identity; then double attacker-controlled size or work and remain inside the admission budget. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Bind negotiated identities, algorithms, public values, roles, and transcript hashes into derivation context.
